capability: structural-reveal discrimination — isolate a line whose bond joins two previously-separate regions of the entity graph
claim: A bond between two STANDING entities sitting in separate graph components is a structural reveal; the interior connectivity channel (bridgeSurprise) fires maximally on it and ~0 on surface-matched controls (adjacent re-bond, fresh entity), while the mass-based one-surprise (bayesBits) does NOT isolate it (ranks fresh-mass lines above it); the connectivity reading is invariant to a surface emphasis cue.
stimulus_shape: text · discrimination+invariance · L3 · whole-reading · target CON
verdict: CONFIRMED — controls dark (adj=0, fresh=0), bridge unique-argmax on the reveal (1.0), bayes argmax elsewhere (gap real), surface-invariant (unmarked==marked)
mechanism: connectivity collapse over the CON/SIG bond graph: a bond's endpoints in different components -> 1, same-component geodesic delta -> (delta-1)/Dinf, adjacent -> 0; endpoints resolved through the CAUSAL SYN-merge identity quotient (the engine's own union-find, restricted to sentIdx<=cursor) so a reveal that names a standing entity by a new surface form is seen
fix_layer: interior (genome) — new src/core/bridge.js, exported from src/core/index.js beside surpriseAt; wired into src/perceiver/reading.js as an OPT-IN channel readingAt({bridge:true}) so the default path is byte-identical
root_cause_of_gap: the mass one-surprise (surprise.js) has no atom for connectivity — a bond among standing entities is a tiny mass deposit; the structural convergence leaves no trace in the figure field. The existing diagnostic (scripts/reveal-discrimination.mjs bridgeSurprise) located this but lived only in the read-only harness AND keyed on raw surface ids, so it MISSED short-name reveals and faked a 2-hop separation on adjacent controls.
parity: npm test 649->658 (9-test lock added), default readingAt byte-identical (bridge opt-in)
generalization: 194/194 lines of the original reveal-stimulus.json reproduce the prior raw-id bridge column EXACTLY (no regression); coref-resolution is a strict superset — identical where reveals use full names, strictly better on short-name reveals
omnimodal: CONFIRMED on a pure-operator log (no text/parser): cross-component bond -> 1.0, adjacent -> 0, SYN-coref reveal -> 1.0 — the mechanism reads only operators, so any organ emitting CON/SIG/SYN inherits it
scope_holds: any modality whose organ emits bonds (CON/SIG) and identity merges (SYN); structural reveals that join graph regions; reveals naming a standing entity by a new surface form
scope_fails: a reveal carried by NO bond (a pure predication/contradiction leaves connectivity unchanged — the enacted significance loop's province); convergence the weak SVO parser does not draw as a bond or shared object (front-end limit, not the channel's)

capability: signal-derived novelty reserve — the protention tracks the recent novelty rate instead of a hand-rolled constant
claim: At matched prior MASS, the reader's surprise at a genuine newcomer should be LOWER after a burst of newcomers and HIGHER after a drought; the fixed reserve 1/(M+1) is blind to this, so at matched mass the newcomer reserve is identical across novelty-dense, novelty-mid and novelty-barren contexts. A signal-derived reserve makes the matched-mass newcomer reserve monotonic in novelty rate.
stimulus_shape: text+music+neutral · discrimination · L3 · window · target EVA/REC
verdict: CONFIRMED — fixed: matched reserveFig identical 0.2537 (gap), control separates by mass (alive); signal: matched monotonic dense 0.50 > mid 0.20 > barren 0.05, control sane, music+text both separate (omnimodal). 8/8 blind checks.
mechanism: the reserved AMPLITUDE = sum over distinct newcomer births before the cursor of gamma^(at-1-birth) — the gamma-decayed recent newcomer-arrival rate on the field's own mass scale (core/surprise.js noveltyReserve) — fed through the SAME fixed Born step (surpriseAt / pNovel / forwardDist). Context enters at the amplitude; the law (square, normalize) stays put.
fix_layer: interior (genome) — new noveltyReserve in src/core/surprise.js, exported from src/core/index.js beside surpriseAt; wired into src/perceiver/reading.js for the figure field (surprisal/forward) and the proposition field (significance) as an OPT-IN behind opts.reserve='signal' (default 'fixed'), so the default path is byte-identical
root_cause_of_gap: NOVELTY_RESERVE = 1.0 is a hand-rolled constant — the brief's named exemplar. The reserve probability 1/(M+1) is a function of accumulated mass (≈ step count), blind to whether newcomers have actually been arriving, so the reader grows equally certain that nothing new will come whether it just saw six newcomers or none
parity: full suite byte-identical with the flag off (default readingAt unchanged); suite 676->681 (+5-test lock); merged clean over main's bridgeSurprise channel (orthogonal opt: opts.reserve vs opts.bridge)
generalization: off-by-default → no confirmed capability regressed (whole suite green); the SAME interior change lifts the discrimination in three readings (neutral, text, music); promotion to default deferred until shown to lift the surf / enacted-loop readings too
omnimodal: CONFIRMED in two organs — text (parseText) AND music (ingestMusic) both show fixed dense≈barren (Δ=0) and signal dense>barren at matched figure-mass; the change reads only INS births, so any organ emitting INS inherits it
scope_holds: the figure field (surprisal/forward protention) and the proposition field (significance reserve); any organ emitting INS; default recency horizon
scope_fails: not promoted to default; the entity-horizon path counts births from the unfiltered firstIns (the default recency horizon admits all, so unaffected); the reserve still self-floors at a numerical epsilon at the very opening
